data_flows.description: Client issues LDP requests (POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE) to Solid pod resources with NIP-98 authentication headers
LDP server delegates access control evaluation to WAC engine against did:nostr ACL rules
Successful mutations recorded in git-backed pod storage with content-addressed history

guides.description: Solid Linked Data Platform protocol: container management, resource CRUD, content-negotiation, slug handling, containment triples

NIP-98 HTTP authentication: every mutation request must carry a valid Schnorr-signed authorization event. Schnorr verification is behind the off-by-default nip98-schnorr feature; replay protection is not yet enforced on this tier (see closeout T4).
Content-negotiation rules: Accept/Content-Type headers govern Turtle/JSON-LD/N-Triples serialisation. Unsupported media types return 415.

Git-marks invariant: every pod mutation produces a git commit. Commit history is append-only; force-push is rejected. Export via git-fast-export preserves marks. Git logic lives in the separate solid-pod-rs-git crate.

BIP-341 Taproot block-trail rules: high-value mutations optionally produce a Taproot commitment for external auditability. PLANNED — no taproot implementation exists in solid-pod-rs today (documentation only).

sensors.description: WAC ACL evaluation tests: verify that acl:Read, acl:Write, acl:Control, and acl:Append permissions are correctly enforced against did:nostr agent identities

Schnorr signature verification: NIP-98 auth events validated for correct pubkey, timestamp freshness, and URL binding. Exercises the nip98-schnorr feature path; replay rejection is not yet part of this tier.
Content-type validation: LDP endpoints negotiate Turtle, JSON-LD, N-Triples. 415 returned for unsupported types. Round-trip fidelity verified.
Git history integrity: every mutation produces a commit, history is append-only, git-fast-export produces valid marks, force-push attempts are rejected

BIP-341 Taproot commitment verification: block-trail anchors are valid, Merkle path resolves, external audit can independently verify commitment. PLANNED — pairs with the planned block-trail-rules guide; no implementation exists yet.
